---

## Why this is a no-op

The duplicate-document signal that fired v1 is **still live**. Consolidation per v1 §4 has not landed. v1's exit gate — "do NOT re-fire on the same duplicate-document signal if v1's analysis still applies, and do NOT re-fire on a readiness-report `.md` write alone" — applies to this fire as well. v1 §4 edit proposals remain canonical. Re-running §1–§5 of the workflow would produce identical proposals.

What v6 adds beyond v5: (a) post-v5 file-mtime evidence, (b) confirmation that the v5 HALT recommendation was not honored, and (c) the cross-day rollover of the loop.

## Post-v5 evidence (between v5 22:16 and v6 fire ~00:59)

```
22:20  planning-artifacts/architecture.md          edited (canonical hyphen dir)
22:23  planning_artifacts/ux-spec.md               edited (UNDERSCORE — regression)
22:28  planning-artifacts/epics.md                 edited (canonical hyphen dir)
22:31  planning-artifacts/implementation-readiness-report-2026-04-24.md  written (NEW)
23:51  eusolicit-docs/implementation-artifacts/    mtime updated (story work continuing)
```

Two interpretations of the canonical-dir edits at 22:20 / 22:28:

1. **Consolidation work in progress.** Some agent or human is actively editing the canonical hyphen-dir copies. Good direction; v1 §4 may be partially landing.
2. **Independent edits unrelated to consolidation.** The hyphen-dir files have always been the active ones; these are normal updates and the underscore-dir / archive cleanup is still pending.

v6 does not attempt to disambiguate — both interpretations leave the v1 success criteria unfinished. The underscore-dir regression at 22:23 (post-v5) is unambiguous: **ACT-V5-01 was not addressed**, and whatever skill/template/tool writes to the underscore path is still active.

The 22:31 readiness-report write is the most plausible immediate trigger for this fire, matching v3's hypothesis. **ACT-V3-01 (exclude readiness-report writes from drift triggers) has not landed** — the loop continues to be self-feeding.

---

## Pathological loop status — now cross-day

| Metric                              | After v2 | After v3 | After v4 | After v5 | **After v6**          |
| ----------------------------------- | -------- | -------- | -------- | -------- | --------------------- |
| Total fires on this signal          | 2        | 3        | 4        | 5        | **6**                 |
| Substantive fires                   | 1 (v1)   | 1 (v1)   | 1 (v1)   | 1 (v1)   | **1 (v1)**            |
| Redundancy rate                     | 50%      | 66%      | 75%      | 80%      | **83%**               |
| Mitigation landed                   | No       | No       | No       | No       | **No**                |
| HALT recommendation honored         | n/a      | n/a      | n/a      | Issued   | **Not honored**       |
| Calendar days spanned               | 1        | 1        | 1        | 1        | **2 (rolled to 4-25)** |

The redundancy rate continues asymptoting toward 100%. v5's explicit HALT recommendation was issued ~3 hours before this fire and was not honored, which means the orchestrator either (a) does not surface proposal recommendations to operators, (b) has no manual override path available, or (c) was not read in time. Any of those is itself a finding.

## Carry-forward action list (verbatim from prior proposals; nothing closed)

- **v1 §4** edit proposals (PRD/architecture/UX consolidation, `planning_artifacts/` deletion, archive README, duplicate-epic resolution) — **OPEN**
- **ACT-V2-carry, ACT-V3-01..04, ACT-V4-01..03, ACT-V5-01** — **all OPEN**

v6 adds nothing new to the project edit proposal set. v6 adds the following observation to the orchestrator-team escalation:

- **ACT-V6-01 (P0)** — Treat the v5→v6 transition as definitive evidence that recommendation-only HALTs do not stop this loop. The next fire (v7) must be prevented by an enforced mitigation — either (a) a hard kill-switch on `correct-course` for this project until ACT-V3-01 lands, or (b) a debounce that suppresses fires when the previous N=2 fires were no-ops. Recommendations alone are insufficient.
- **ACT-V6-02 (P1)** — File a postmortem for the 2026-04-24/25 drift-detection incident covering: trigger source not logged, no-op fires not suppressed, HALT recommendation not actionable, underscore-dir writer not identified. Six fires on one signal in <12h is the kind of incident that should produce a written RCA.

---

## Action — None for the project; **escalating** for the orchestrator team

**For project artifacts (PRD/Epics/Architecture/UX/Stories):** No new edit proposals. v1 §4 remains the canonical change set.

**For the orchestrator team — required emergency mitigations, in priority order:**

1. **HARD HALT** drift-detection on this project until ACT-V3-01 lands. v5's soft-HALT recommendation was not honored; the soft form is no longer credible. If a kill-switch does not exist, treat its absence as the highest-priority fix.
2. **Same orchestrator deploy:** implement debounce — suppress `correct-course` if the previous fire (a) was a no-op AND (b) occurred within the last 4 hours. Cheap; would have prevented v2–v6.
3. **Next sprint:** land ACT-V3-01 (exclude readiness-report writes from drift triggers) AND ACT-V3-02 (wire drift threshold) AND add trigger-event logging so root-cause analysis is not guesswork.
4. **Find and fix the underscore-path writer (ACT-V5-01).** Until that lands, deletion of `eusolicit-docs/planning_artifacts/` will be reverted. Search command: `rg -l 'planning_artifacts[^-]' .` from project root.
5. **File the RCA per ACT-V6-02.**
